data: Meligunis Lipara VII. Lipari. Contrada Diana. Scavo XXXVI in proprieta Zagami (1975-1984)
L. Bernabò Brea e M. Cavalier, Meligunis Lipara VII. Lipari. Contrada Diana. Scavo XXXVI in proprieta Zagami (1975-1984) (Palermo, 1994)
note small open omega, broken bar alpha, standard sigma
Part of a structure 'di eta ellenistica', perhaps 3rd century BCE.
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana
Bernabò Brea and Cavalier, 1994, Meligunis Lipara VII. Lipari. Contrada Diana. Scavo XXXVI in proprieta Zagami (1975-1984)
Museo Archeologico Regionale Eoliano "Luigi Bernab� Brea"
museo archeologico regionale eoliano "luigi bernab� brea"
3rd-2nd century BCE (editors suggest C3 due to quality of lettering)
Bernabò Brea, 1982, Iscrizioni funerarie di cittadini eleati a Lipari
Note pi with short right hasta
The inscription is between two irregular engraved guide lines
The inscription is between two thin engraved guide lines
Line 1, rho 65mm, line 2, chi 69mm, rho 65mm. Sign on the reverse 29.5cm
Line 1: omicron 45mm, Line 2: omicron 40mm, Line 3: omicron 48mm
The inscription currently visible is related to the reuse of the stele: it is in fact placed within a lowered band, probably created to erase an older text.
uncial omega and lunate sigma; standard epsilon
The first letter looks like a reversed N; use of lunate sigma
1st century BCE (but the original punic? inscr. and the stele are 4th — 3rd century BCE.

1st century BCE (Brea thinks second
                                half, but this is circular arg. on basis of names)
1st century BCE (Brea thinks second half, but this is circular arg. on basis of names)

1st century BCE — 1st century CE (Brea
                                uncertain, but prob. 'di eta seriore rispetto alla massa' inscr. gr.)
